title: Xenial Velvety Slovakian Fish Stew
description: A rich and velvety fish stew featuring brandy, perfect for a date night, with a resinous flavor profile that is distinctly Slovakian. This pescatarian-friendly dish is sure to impress.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the brandy over medium heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes, cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cod fillets, fish stock, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, stir gently to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and continue to simmer, uncovered, for an additional 5 minutes, until the stew has thickened slightly.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
